reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully encodes the informal claim. (1) `[Field k] [PerfectField k]` matches "perfect field k"; (2) `{p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime] [CharP k p]` parameterizes over a prime p with `CharP k p`, which is equivalent to saying k has characteristic p > 0 (for a field, the characteristic is 0 or prime, so "p > 0" = "p prime"). (3) `RatFunc k` is Mathlib's field of rational functions in one variable over k, matching F = k(t). (4) `[Field E] [Algebra (RatFunc k) E] [FiniteDimensional (RatFunc k) E]` exactly says E is a finite extension of F. (5) The conclusion `∃ α : E, (RatFunc k)⟮α⟯ = ⊤` uses the `IntermediateField.adjoin` notation `F⟮α⟯` and `⊤` denotes the whole field E as an intermediate field, so this says E = F(α), i.e., E is generated by one element α over F. All hypotheses and conclusion match the informal statement without adding or omitting anything essential.
